name: Australia
description: Home to 8 of the world's top 100 universities, Australia combines world-class education with an incredible lifestyle. Strong post-study work options and a clear pathway to permanent residency make it a top choice for Indian students.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Tuition Fee of ELICOS: AUD 300 to AUD 400 per week
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Contact institution for AUD fees
tuitionFees.phd: AUD 28,000–40,000 per year; domestic rate scholarships often available

scholarships.eligibility: Citizens of eligible developing countries; government-funded

scholarships.eligibility: Domestic and international students studying in regional Australia

scholarships.name: University International Scholarships

scholarships.eligibility: Higher Degree by Research students (PhD, Masters by Research)

OSHC (Overseas Student Health Cover) for the duration of study

workPermit.notes: Regional study boosts post-study work rights by 1–2 extra years

languageRequirements.notes: English language proficiency required for all programs

topUniversities.name: Offered by universities and private colleges
Offered by private colleges and some of the major universities
Type of educational institutes in Australia

faqs.question: Can Indian students work while studying in Australia?
faqs.answer: Yes, student visa holders can work up to 48 hours per fortnight during the academic semester and unlimited hours during scheduled breaks.

faqs.question: What is the PSW visa in Australia?
faqs.answer: The Temporary Graduate Visa (subclass 485) allows international graduates to live and work in Australia for 2–4 years after completing their studies.

faqs.question: Is IELTS mandatory for Australian universities?
faqs.answer: Most universities require an IELTS score of 6.0–6.5 (overall). Some accept alternative tests like PTE, TOEFL, or Duolingo.

faqs.question: What are the popular intakes in Australia?
faqs.answer: The main intakes are February (Semester 1) and July (Semester 2). Some institutions also offer November intake.

name: Belgium
description: Located at the heart of Europe, Belgium is home to some of the continent's oldest universities and serves as the political capital of the EU. Excellent for students interested in international relations, law, and business.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Quality Education: Home to top-ranked universities like KU Leuven and Ghent University, University of Antwerp, Thomas More - University of Applied Science. Multilingual Programs: Offers courses in English, Dutch, and French. Diverse Fields: Strong in engineering, business, arts, and social sciences. Cultural Exposure: Immerse in a rich, multilingual cultural environment. Affordable Tuition: Generally lower tuition fees compared to other Western European countries. Cost of Living: Living expenses vary by city, with Brussels being pricier. Vibrant Student Life: Active international community with clubs and activities. Central Location: Easy travel access to neighboring European countries. Student Visa Required: Non-EU students need a visa, including proof of enrollment and financial means.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Students need to first obtain a visa before they can proceed with online enrolment at the university, where paying the tuition fee is one of the last steps.
tuitionFees.phd: Typically funded through FWO, FNRS grants, or university fellowships

name: Canada
description: Consistently ranked as one of the best countries for quality of life, Canada offers top-tier education with clear pathways to permanent residency. The PGWP makes it a top choice for Indian students seeking long-term immigration.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: CAD 15,000–40,000 per year (varies by province and program)
tuitionFees.postgraduate: CAD 12,000–35,000 per year; MBA programs CAD 30,000–60,000
tuitionFees.phd: CAD 7,000–25,000 per year; many include research funding

scholarships.eligibility: Doctoral students; exceptional academic excellence and leadership

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 24 hours per week off-campus; unlimited on-campus during semester; full-time during scheduled breaks

workPermit.notes: PGWP is a direct pathway to Canadian Permanent Residency (Express Entry)

languageRequirements.notes: English language proficiency required for all programs

faqs.question: What is the PGWP in Canada?
faqs.answer: The Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) allows graduates to work in Canada for up to 3 years. It is a major pathway to Canadian Permanent Residency.

faqs.question: Can I bring my family to Canada as a student?
faqs.answer: Yes, spouses of international students can apply for an open work permit, and children can attend school in Canada.

faqs.question: How long does it take to get a Canadian student permit?
faqs.answer: Processing typically takes 4–8 weeks for online applications. Biometrics may be required for certain nationalities.

faqs.question: What is the SDS (Student Direct Stream) in Canada?
faqs.answer: SDS is a faster processing stream for students from eligible countries including India. Applications are usually processed within 20 working days.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: France is the only country in Europe to offer this benefit. All students are eligible on a case-to-case basis to apply for assistance and receive subsidy on cost of accommodation from 30% to 50% of the rent from the French Government.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: French classes are provided by many Government organizations for free. Enrolment fees are typically around €5 to €10. Duration remains 2 to 3 hours a day. name: Germany
description: The engine of Europe's economy. Germany is globally revered for engineering and technology, offering zero or extremely low tuition fees at public universities — even for international students.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: €0 tuition at public universities (semester administration fee of €150–€350 only)
tuitionFees.postgraduate: €0 at most public universities; €5,000–€20,000 at private institutions
tuitionFees.phd: Free at public universities; doctoral candidates often receive a stipend

scholarships.name: DAAD Scholarship (German Academic Exchange Service)
scholarships.eligibility: Postgraduate and doctoral students; excellent academic record

scholarships.name: Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Socially engaged students with excellent academics

scholarships.name: Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Students with high academic achievement and civic engagement

scholarships.name: University Excellence Scholarships (Deutschlandstipendium)
scholarships.eligibility: Top students; jointly funded by government and private sponsors

requirements: Valid passport (valid for at least 12 months beyond intended stay)
visaRequirements.processingTime: 8–12 weeks; apply at least 3 months before departure

workPermit.duringStudy: 120 full days or 240 half-days per year (approx. 20 hours/week); €8–12/hour typical wage
workPermit.postStudy: 18-month job-seeker visa
workPermit.notes: Opportunity Card (Chancenkarte) available for skilled graduates seeking German employment

languageRequirements.notes: Some programs taught in English; German B2 required for German-taught programs

faqs.question: Is studying in Germany really free?
faqs.answer: Public universities in Germany charge only a semester fee of €150–€350 for administration. There is no tuition fee for most programs, even for international students.

faqs.question: Do I need to know German to study in Germany?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Many master's programs are offered in English. However, knowing German is very helpful for daily life and increases job prospects.

faqs.question: What is the blocked account requirement for Germany?
faqs.answer: A blocked account (Sperrkonto) of €11,904/year (€992/month) is required to show financial sufficiency for your German student visa.

faqs.question: Can I stay in Germany after graduation?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can apply for an 18-month job-seeker visa (Aufenthaltserlaubnis zur Arbeitssuche) after completing your degree.

name: Hungary
description: Hungary is a top destination for medical education in Europe, with WHO-recognized programs and affordable fees. Its vibrant capital Budapest offers a rich student life.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Universities charge comparatively low tuition fees. At the same time, student life in Hungary is very affordable. Considering accommodation, food, transport and other things, you can get by on less than 600 euros per month in Budapest (the capital), and on less than 500 euros per month in the smaller cities.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Visa fee will be EUR 110. No visa fee waiver for this category of travel except for holders of diplomatic and service passports.

name: Japan
description: A global leader in technology and innovation, Japan offers cutting-edge research opportunities and a unique cultural experience. Government scholarships like MEXT make it accessible for international students.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Only a few scholarships in Japan cover the entire cost of studying. At the same time, the majority only aim to cover a portion of the tuition fees and living expenses. This means it’s important to calculate the entire cost of your studies abroad well ahead of time and see how much financial help you need to afford it.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Like in many other countries, the cost of living in Japan is different depending on the area where you live. Expenses in urban areas like Tokyo are generally higher compared to rural areas. In terms of housing, the average cost in Japan for students is around 260 EURO per month, but in Tokyo, it goes up to 340 EURO. Another thing to remember is that in big cities, it’s harder to find accommodation close to schools which means you will spend more on transport.
tuitionFees.phd: Often supported through MEXT or JASSO scholarships

scholarships.name: Financial Aid and Scholarships in Japan

name: United Kingdom
description: Experience academic prestige with shorter course durations. The UK is a global leader in quality education with the 2–3 year Graduate Route Visa making it one of the most sought-after study destinations.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: £9,250–£30,000 per year (varies by program and university)
tuitionFees.phd: £5,000–£15,000 per year; many are fully funded through research councils

scholarships.name: Chevening Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Outstanding academics with leadership potential; government-backed

scholarships.name: Commonwealth Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Citizens of Commonwealth countries for postgraduate study

scholarships.name: GREAT Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Indian students for postgraduate courses at UK universities

scholarships.name: University Merit Awards
scholarships.eligibility: High-achieving international applicants (varies by university)

requirements: Valid passport with at least 6 months validity beyond your course end date
Unconditional offer letter (CAS) from a UK licensed sponsor university
Proof of English proficiency (IELTS 6.0+ or equivalent)
Financial evidence: £1,334/month for London or £1,023/month outside London
TB test certificate (if applicable for your nationality)
UK Student Visa application fee: £490 (from outside UK)

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 20 hours per week during term time; full-time during official vacation periods
workPermit.postStudy: Graduate Route Visa: 2 years for degree graduates; 3 years for PhD graduates
workPermit.notes: No IELTS required for the Graduate Route if already on a Student Visa

languageRequirements.notes: English language proficiency required for all programs

topUniversities.name: Bristol, University of Glasgow, University of Birmingham

faqs.question: What is the UK Graduate Route Visa?
faqs.answer: The Graduate Route allows international students to stay in the UK for 2 years (3 for PhD graduates) after completing their degree to work or look for work.

faqs.question: Do I need a CAS number for a UK Student Visa?
faqs.answer: Yes, you need a Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) from your UK university before applying for a Student Visa.

faqs.question: How much bank balance is needed for a UK Student Visa?
faqs.answer: You must show £1,334/month for up to 9 months for London, or £1,023/month for outside London, plus your full first-year tuition fees.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying in the UK?
faqs.answer: Yes, most student visa holders can work up to 20 hours per week during term time and full-time during official vacation periods.

name: United States
description: The US is home to the world's highest number of top-ranked universities, offering unparalleled academic flexibility, research opportunities, and career prospects through OPT and STEM extensions.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Visa Fees Payment can be done through IMPS, NEFT and Challan.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Pay visa application processing fee of $185 and Schedule an interview (Link - https://www.usvisascheduling.com/) Students scheduling Interview for US visa are required to schedule for two dates-
tuitionFees.phd: Often fully funded with stipends for research assistants (TA/RA)

scholarships.name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
scholarships.eligibility: Outstanding academic record; leadership qualities; government-sponsored

scholarships.name: Hubert H. Humphrey Fellowship
scholarships.eligibility: Mid-career professionals from eligible countries

scholarships.name: University Merit Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: High GPA, GRE/GMAT scores; varies by institution

scholarships.name: Graduate Teaching/Research Assistantships (TA/RA)
scholarships.eligibility: Postgraduate students; competitive selection

requirements: Valid passport (must be valid for at least 6 months beyond stay)
Form I-20 from your SEVP-certified US institution
SEVIS fee payment receipt ($350 for F-1 students)
Proof of financial support (bank statements, affidavit of support)
Academic transcripts and English test scores (IELTS/TOEFL)
Evidence of ties to home country (intent to return after studies)

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 20 hours per week on-campus; Curricular Practical Training (CPT) for off-campus internships
workPermit.postStudy: Optional Practical Training (OPT): 1 year; STEM OPT Extension: additional 24 months (total 3 years)
workPermit.notes: H-1B visa lottery is the primary long-term path to US employment authorization

languageRequirements.notes: English language proficiency required for all programs

topUniversities.name: Elite Colleges very selective in admission, academic excellence, known for Business programs
Virginia Tech Language and Culture Institute

faqs.question: What is F-1 OPT?
faqs.answer: Optional Practical Training (OPT) allows F-1 students to work in a job related to their major for up to 12 months. STEM graduates can apply for a 24-month extension.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to study in the USA?
faqs.answer: Tuition ranges from $20,000–$60,000/year at private universities and $10,000–$30,000/year at public universities for out-of-state students.

faqs.question: What are the SAT/GRE/GMAT requirements?
faqs.answer: Requirements vary by program. Most graduate programs require GRE or GMAT. Undergraduate admissions often consider SAT/ACT scores.

faqs.question: Can Indian students get scholarships in the USA?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many universities offer merit scholarships, and there are federal programs like Fulbright. Assistantships (TA/RA) also provide stipends for graduate students.
